name: speculos

metadata.name: speculos
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Ledger Blue, Stax, Flex and Nano S/S+/X application emulator

documentation:
# Speculos

[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/LedgerHQ/speculos/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/LedgerHQ/speculos)
[![lgtm](https://img.shields.io/lgtm/alerts/g/LedgerHQ/speculos.svg?logo=lgtm&logoWidth=18)](https://lgtm.com/projects/g/LedgerHQ/speculos/alerts/)

![screenshot btc nano s](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/LedgerHQ/speculos/master/docs/screenshot-api-nanos-btc.png)

The goal of this project is to emulate Ledger Nano S/S+, Nano X, Blue, Flex and Stax apps on
standard desktop computers, without any hardware device. More information can
be found here in the
[documentation website](https://ledgerhq.github.io/speculos) (or in the
[docs/ folder](docs/) directly).

Usage example:

```shell
./speculos.py apps/btc.elf --model nanos
# ... and open a browser on http://127.0.0.1:5000
```

## Installation

### From Python `pypi` packages

The easiest, stable way to install Speculos is with `pip`:

```
pip install speculos
```

It is advised to use Python virtualenv, otherwise admin rights will probably be
necessary.


### From sources

Installing Speculos from sources is a bit heavier and, depending on the platform,
complex, due to all the dependency needed for compiling the emulator.

On Debian (10 or later) or Ubuntu (18.04 or later):

```
sudo apt install \
    git cmake gcc-arm-linux-gnueabihf libc6-dev-armhf-cross gdb-multiarch \
    python3-pyqt5 python3-construct python3-flask-restful python3-jsonschema \
    python3-mnemonic python3-pil python3-pyelftools python3-requests \
    qemu-user-static libvncserver-dev

# from the root directory of the source repository
pip install .
```

Dependency management will vary on other platforms; using Docker images and/or WSL
should facilitate the installation.

## Bugs and contributions

Feel free to open issues and create pull requests on this GitHub repository.

The `master` branch is protected to disable force pushing. Contributions should
be made through pull requests, which are reviewed by @LedgerHQ members before
being merged to `master`:

- @LedgerHQ members can create branches directly on the repository (if member of
  a team with write access to the repository)
- External contributors should fork the repository


## Limitations

There is absolutely no guarantee that apps will have the same behavior on
hardware devices and Speculos, though the differences are limited.

### Syscalls

The emulator handles only a few syscalls made by common apps. For instance,
syscalls related to app install, firmware update or OS info can't be
implemented.

Invalid syscall parameters might throw an exception on a real device while
being ignored on Speculos.
Notably, this is the case for application allowed derivation path and curve and
application settings flags which are enforced by the device OS, but ignored by
Speculos.

### Memory alignment

Attempts to perform unaligned accesses when not allowed (eg. dereferencing a
misaligned pointer) will cause an alignment fault on a Ledger Nano S device but
not on Speculos. Note that such unaligned accesses are supported by other
Ledger devices.

Following code crashes on LNS device, but not on Speculos nor on other devices.
```
uint8_t buffer[20];
for (int i = 0; i < 20; i++) {
    buffer[i] = i;
}
uint32_t display_value = *((uint32_t*) (buffer + 1));
PRINTF("display_value: %d\n", display_value);
```

### Watchdog

NanoX, Flex and Stax devices use an internal watchdog enforcing usage of regular
calls to `io_seproxyhal_io_heartbeat();`. This watchdog is not emulated on
Speculos.


## Security

Apps can make arbitrary Linux system calls (and use QEMU
[semihosting](docs/user/semihosting.md) features), thus don't run Speculos on
untrusted apps.

It's worth noting that the syscall implementation (`src/`) doesn't expect
malicious input. By the way, in Speculos, there is no privilege separation
between the app and the syscalls. This doesn't reflect the security of the
firmware on hardware devices where app and OS isolation is enforced.

Speculos is not part of Ledger bug bounty program.


## Are you developing a Nano App as an external developer?

For a smooth and quick integration:
- Follow the developers documentation on the [Developer Portal](https://developers.ledger.com/docs/nano-app/introduction/) and
- [Go on Discord](https://developers.ledger.com/discord-pro/) to chat with developer support and the developer community.

don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: speculos.mcu.button_tcp
documentation:
TCP server to emulate buttons:

- L/R: press left/right button
- l/r: release left/right button

Usage example:

    $ echo -n RrRrRr | nc -nv 127.0.0.1 1235
    $ echo -n LRlr | nc -nv 127.0.0.1 1235

functions.name: speculos.client.screenshot_equal
documentation: Compare two images and return True if they are equal.

methods.name: speculos.client.Api.get_next_event
documentation:
A subset of the event stream format is recognized by this function and the event is expected to be encoded in
JSON.

https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#parsing-an-event-stream

methods.name: speculos.client.Api.wait_for_text_event
documentation: Wait until an event containing the specified text is received.

methods.name: speculos.client.SpeculosClient.start
documentation:
Start speculos to emulate the application. `stop` must be called to ensure proper
termination of speculos.
Note that context `with` statement can be used with `SpeculosClient` instance instead of
using `start` and `stop` methods.

methods.name: speculos.client.SpeculosClient.stop
documentation: Terminate speculos instance started with `start` method.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.ocr.OCR.analyze_bitmap
documentation:
data contain information about the latest displayed bitmap.
Since unified SDK, speculos added the displayed character to data.
For older SKD versions, legacy behaviour is used: parsing internal
fonts to find a matching bitmap.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.TimeTickerDaemon.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Ticker Daemon
The goal of this daemon is to emulate the time spent in the application by sending it
ticker events at a regular interval TICKER_DELAY.
This daemon can be paused and resumed through its API to stop the flow of time in the MCU

```
param add_tick

The callback function to add a Ticker Event to the packet manager

type add_tick

Backend
```

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.TimeTickerDaemon.pause
documentation: Pause time emulation done by the daemon, no ticker event will be sent until resume

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.TimeTickerDaemon.resume
documentation: Resume time emulation done by the daemon

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.TimeTickerDaemon._wait_if_time_paused
documentation: Internal function to handle the pause

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.TimeTickerDaemon.run
documentation: Main thread function

documentation: Send a packet to the app.

Append a packet to the queue of packets to be sent.

This function is meant to called by other threads.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SocketHelper.add_tick
documentation: Request sending of a ticker event to the app

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SeProxyHal.can_read

Handle packet sent by the app.

This function is called thanks to a screen QSocketNotifier.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SeProxyHal.handle_button
documentation: Forward button press/release from the GUI to the app.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SeProxyHal.handle_finger
documentation: Forward finger press/release from the GUI to the app.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SeProxyHal.handle_wait
documentation: Wait for a specified delay, taking account real time seen by the app.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.seproxyhal.SeProxyHal.to_app
documentation:
Forward raw APDU to the app.

Packets can be forwarded directly to the SE thanks to
SephTag.CAPDU_EVENT, but it doesn't work with messages are larger
than G_io_seproxyhal_spi_buffer. Emulating a basic USB stack is more
reliable, see issue #11.

documentation: Check if there are some duplicated pairs (same values) and merge them.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.bpp4_to_values
documentation:
Expand each bytes of data into 2 quartets.
Return an array of values (from 0x00 to 0x0F)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.values_to_bpp4
documentation:
Takes values (assumed from 0x00 to 0x0F) in data and returns an array
of bytes containing quartets with values concatenated.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.bpp1_to_values
documentation:
Expand each bytes of data into 8 bits, each stored in a byte
Return an array of values (containing bytes values 0 or 1)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.values_to_bpp1
documentation:
Takes values (bytes containing 0 or 1) in data and returns an array
of bytes containing bits concatenated.
(first pixel is bit 10000000 of first byte)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.bpp_to_values
documentation:
Expand each bytes of data, containing pixels (4BPP or 1BPP)
Return an array of values (0x00 to 0x0F or 0x00 to 0x01)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.values_to_bpp
documentation:
Takes values (0x00 to 0x0F or 0x00 to 0x01) in data and returns an
array of bytes containing pixels concatenated.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.encode_pass2
documentation:
Encode tuples containing (repeat, val) into packed values.
(empty method intended to be overwritten)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.decode_pass2
documentation:
Decode packed bytes into array of tuples containing (repeat, value).
(empty method intended to be overwritten)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.encode_pass1
documentation:
Encode array of values using 'normal' RLE.
Return an array of tuples containing (repeat, val)

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.decode_pass1
documentation:
Decode array of tuples containing (repeat, val).
Return an array of values.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.encode
documentation:
Input: array of packed pixels
- convert to an array of values
- convert to tuples of (repeat, value)
- encode using custom RLE
Output: array of compressed bytes

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.decode
documentation:
Input: array of compressed bytes
- decode to pairs using custom RLE
- convert the tuples of (repeat, value) to values
- convert to an array of packed pixels
Output: array of packed pixels

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomBase.get_encoded_size
documentation:
Return the encoded size.
(to be overwritten if something more complex than len() need to be done)
Class handling regular RLE encoding (1st pass)
Regular RLE encoding

classes.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom1

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom1.encode_pass2
documentation:
Encode tuples containing (repeat, val) into packed values.
The generated bytes will contain:
RRRRVVVV
- RRRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 16 repeat counts
- VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom1.decode_pass2
documentation:
Decode packed bytes into array of tuples containing (repeat, value).
The bytes provided contains:
RRRRVVVV
- RRRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 16 repeat counts
- VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

Class handling custom RLE encoding (2nd pass)
The generated bytes will contain:
* for 1 BPP: RRRRRRRV
  - RRRRRRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 256 repeat counts
  - V: value of the 1BPP pixel (0 or 1)
* for 4 BPP: RRRRVVVV
- RRRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 16 repeat counts
- VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel
Custom RLE encoding: pack repeat count & value into 1 byte

classes.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom2

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom2.encode_pass2
documentation:
Encode tuples containing (repeat, val) into packed values.
The generated bytes will contain:
1XXXXXXX
0XXXXXXX
With:
  * 1RRRRRRR
    - RRRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets (max=127+1)
  * 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom2.decode_pass2
documentation:
Decode packed bytes into array of tuples containing (repeat, value).
The bytes provided contains:
1XXXXXXX
0XXXXXXX
With:
  * 1RRRRRRR
    - RRRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets
  * 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

Class handling custom RLE encoding (2nd pass)
The generated bytes will contain:
1XXXXXXX
0XXXXXXX
With:
* 1RRRRRRR
    - RRRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets
* 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel
Custom RLE encoding: pack repeat count & value into 1 byte +
- white handling

classes.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom3

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom3.encode_pass2
documentation:
Encode tuples containing (repeat, val) into packed values.
The generated bytes will contain:
11RRRRRR
10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ
0RRRVVVV
With:
  * 11RRRRRR
    - RRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets (max=63+1)
  * 10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ QQQQ0000
    - RR is repeat count - 3 of quartets (max=3+3 => 6 quartets)
    - VVVV: value of 1st 4BPP pixel
    - WWWW: value of 2nd 4BPP pixel
    - XXXX: value of 3rd 4BPP pixel
    - YYYY: value of 4th 4BPP pixel
    - ZZZZ: value of 5th 4BPP pixel
    - QQQQ: value of 6th 4BPP pixel
  * 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom3.decode_pass2
documentation:
Decode packed bytes into array of tuples containing (repeat, value).
The bytes provided contains:
11RRRRRR
10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ
0RRRVVVV
With:
  * 11RRRRRR
    - RRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets (max=63+1)
  * 10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ QQQQ0000
    - RR is repeat count - 3 of quartets (max=3+3 => 6 quartets)
    - VVVV: value of 1st 4BPP pixel
    - WWWW: value of 2nd 4BPP pixel
    - XXXX: value of 3rd 4BPP pixel
    - YYYY: value of 4th 4BPP pixel
    - ZZZZ: value of 5th 4BPP pixel
    - QQQQ: value of 6th 4BPP pixel
  * 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel

Class handling custom RLE encoding (2nd pass)
The generated bytes will contain:
11RRRRRR
10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ QQQQ0000
0RRRVVVV
With:
* 11RRRRRR
    - RRRRRRR is repeat count - 1 of White (0xF) quartets (max=63+1)
* 10RRVVVV WWWWXXXX YYYYZZZZ QQQQ0000
    - RR is repeat count - 3 of quartets (max=3+3 => 6 quartets)
    - VVVV: value of 1st 4BPP pixel
    - WWWW: value of 2nd 4BPP pixel
    - XXXX: value of 3rd 4BPP pixel
    - YYYY: value of 4th 4BPP pixel
    - ZZZZ: value of 5th 4BPP pixel
    - QQQQ: value of 6th 4BPP pixel
* 0RRRVVVV
    - RRR: repeat count - 1 => allow to store 1 to 8 repeat counts
    - VVVV: value of the 4BPP pixel
Custom RLE encoding: pack repeat count & value into 1 byte +
- white handling
- copy range of bytes

classes.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomA

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustomA.encode_pass2

Encode Alternance counts between pixels (nb of 0, then nb of 1, etc)
The generated packed values will contain ZZZZOOOO ZZZZOOOO with
- ZZZZ: number of consecutives 0, from 0 to 15
- OOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 15

- OOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 15

The generated bytes will contain Alternances counts ZZZZOOOO with
- ZZZZ: number of consecutives 0, from 0 to 15
- OOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 15
Custom RLE encoding: pack repeat count & value into 1 byte

Decode packed bytes into array of tuples containing (repeat, value).
The generated bytes will contain Alternances counts ZZZZZZZZ OOOOOOOO with
- ZZZZZZZZ: number of consecutives 0, from 0 to 255
- OOOOOOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 255

Class handling custom RLE encoding (2nd pass) for 1 BPP only

- ZZZZZZZZ: number of consecutives 0, from 0 to 255
- OOOOOOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 255
TODO: check the same with 6 bits for repeat count
ZZZZZZOO OOOOZZZZ ZZOOOOOO
- ZZZZZZ: number of consecutives 0, from 0 to 63
- OOOOOO: number of consecutives 1, from 0 to 63
Custom RLE encoding: pack repeat count & value into 1 byte

classes.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom.encode

Try different encoding algorithms to compress 4 or 1BPP pixels.
Input:
- array of packed pixels
- number of bpp (1 or 4)
Output: Tuple containing:
- method used to encode data
- bytes array containing encoded data

methods.name: speculos.mcu.rle_custom.RLECustom.decode
documentation:
Decompress previously encoded data using the provided method.
Input: array of compressed bytes
Output: array of packed pixels
Class handling custom RLE encoding
Entry point for easy RLE encoding/decoding

classes.name: speculos.mcu.display.IODevice

methods.name: speculos.mcu.display.IODevice.file
documentation: Returns the file (Pipe, Socket, ...) tied to this `IODevice`

methods.name: speculos.mcu.display.IODevice.fileno
documentation: Returns the file descriptor of the file tied to this `IODevice`

methods.name: speculos.mcu.display.IODevice.can_read
documentation: Callback used by a notifier to trigger `IODevice` events on given screen

An interface every class implementing application IOs (screens, buttons, APDUs, ...) should
inherit from.

These classes will be managed by a `DisplayNotifier`, which is responsible to redirect events
to the correct `IODevice` instance through its `IODevice.can_read` callback.

classes.name: speculos.mcu.display.FrameBuffer

methods.name: speculos.mcu.display.FrameBuffer.update
documentation: Should be declared as an `@abstractmethod` (and also `class FrameBuffer(ABC):`), but in this
case multiple inheritance in `screen.PaintWidget(FrameBuffer, QWidget)` will break, as both
FrameBuffer and QWidget derive from a different metaclass, and Python cannot figure out which
class builder to use.

A class responsible for managing the graphic screen of the current application.

It updates the screen, takes screenshots, manages colors and such.

classes.name: speculos.mcu.display.GraphicLibrary
documentation:
A class interface defining mandatory method a graphical library must implement.

Currently implemented graphic libraries are `bagl.Bagl` and `nbgl.NBGL`.

classes.name: speculos.mcu.display.Display
documentation:
A class interface for managing the graphic display of an application.

Every display type is composed of two classes:
- A `Display` implementation, which will mostly deal with the graphics
- A `DisplayNotifier`, which will managed the `IOdevice` tied to the running application.

Currently, there are 3 display management type, stored in following modules:
- `screen.py`, displaying the application infos using Qt
- `screen_text.py`, displaying the application infos using the terminal (through `curses`)
- `headless.py`, displaying nothing, although the application interface can still be reached
  through VNC if activated.

classes.name: speculos.mcu.display.DisplayNotifier
documentation:
A class interface for managing the events between an application display and the `IODevice`
subclasses.

Every display type is composed of two classes:
- A `Display` implementation, which will mostly deal with the graphics
- A `DisplayNotifier`, which will managed the `IOdevice` tied to the running application.

Currently, there are 3 display management type, stored in following modules:
- `screen.py`, displaying the application infos using Qt
- `screen_text.py`, displaying the application infos using the terminal (through `curses`)
- `headless.py`, displaying nothing, although the application interface can still be reached
  through VNC if activated.

modules.name: speculos.mcu.transport.nfc
documentation: Forward NFC packets between the MCU and the SE

methods.name: speculos.mcu.transport.nfc.NFC.handle_rapdu
documentation: concatenate apdu chunks into full apdu

modules.name: speculos.mcu.transport.usb
documentation:
Forward USB packets between the MCU and the SE using the (custom) SE Proxy HAL
protocol.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.transport.usb.USBTransport.config
documentation: Parse a config packet. If the endpoint address is set, configure it.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.transport.usb.USBTransport.prepare
documentation: Send or receive a packet chunk.

modules.name: speculos.mcu.finger_tcp
documentation:
TCP server to emulate finger actions on screen:

args are sent in csv format (x,y,pressed) and can be chained.

Usage example:

    $ echo -n 128,300,1 | nc -nv 127.0.0.1 1236
    $ echo -n 128,300,0,110,41,0 | nc -nv 127.0.0.1 1236

modules.name: speculos.mcu.vnc
documentation:
Forward framebuffer to a VNC server (vnc/vnc_server) and receive mouse events.

This VNC server can be tested with Remmina for instance (with color depth set to
true color: 24 bbp).

methods.name: speculos.mcu.vnc.VNC.redraw
documentation: The framebuffer was updated, forward everything to the VNC server.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.vnc.VNC.can_read
documentation: Process a new keyboard or mouse event message from the VNC server.

modules.name: speculos.mcu.automation_server
documentation: Forward screen events (text, x, y) to TCP clients.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.automation_server.AutomationServer.broadcast
documentation: Broadcast an event to each connected client.

variables.name: speculos.__version__
documentation: noqa

methods.name: speculos.mcu.screen.App.mousePressEvent
documentation: Get the mouse location.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.screen.App.closeEvent
documentation:
Called when the window is closed. We save the current window position to
the settings file in order to restore it upon next speculos execution.

methods.name: speculos.mcu.nbgl.NBGL.hal_draw_image_rle
documentation:
Draw a bitmap (4BPP or 1BPP) which has been encoded via custom RLE.
Input:
data contains (check sys_nbgl_front_draw_img_rle in src/bolos/nbgl.c)
- area (sizeof(nbgl_area_t))
- compressed bitmap (buffer_len)
- foreground_color (1 byte)
- nb_skipped_bytes (1 byte)
- character (4 bytes) [added by speculos syscall]

classes.name: speculos.api.events.EventsBroadcaster
documentation: This used to be the 'Automation Server'.

methods.name: speculos.api.apdu.APDUBridge.seph_apdu_callback
documentation:
Called by seph when data is transmitted by the SE. That data should
be the response to a prior APDU request

classes.name: speculos.api.api.ApiRunner
documentation: Run the Speculos API server in a dedicated thread, with a notification when it stops
